g.text: FALSE POSITIVES: Why Leak Reports Lie
Roots are always reachable by definition
Input | Root set | Root set (globals, stack)
Traverse | Follow refs | Follow refs (requires ptr tracking)
Mark | Set mark bit | Record as reachable
Sweep | Free unmarked | REPORT unmarked (no auto-free)
Result | Reclaimed mem | List of potential leaks
Singleton | One-time alloc, freed at exit
Lazy init state | Allocated once on first use
Leak = unfreed AND programmer INTENDED to free
count | usize | How many of this type
Human judgment required — tools can only flag candidates
